---
title: "Titanic Survival Analysis"
author: "xipecast"
date: "2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 14))
titanic <- read_csv("/home/boss/Documents/myDocuments/ProjectsOnLenovo/xipecast-analyst-portfolio/titanic-analysis/data/titanic.csv")
titanic_clean <- titanic %>%
  mutate(Age = ifelse(is.na(Age), median(Age, na.rm = TRUE), Age)) %>%
  mutate(Embarked = ifelse(is.na(Embarked), "S", Embarked)) %>%
  select(-Cabin)
```

## Overview
Analysis of 891 Titanic passengers to determine survival factors.

## Key Findings
| Factor | Finding |
|--------|---------|
| Gender | Women survived at 74.2% vs men at 18.9% |
| Class | 1st class 63% vs 3rd class 24.2% |
| Family | Small families had best odds |
| Fare | Higher fare = better survival |
